name: Endpoints News
notes: Excluded: robots.txt explicitly disallows anthropic-ai/Claude-Web/ClaudeBot. Also returns HTTP 403 to a non-browser User-Agent.

name: GEN - Genetic Engineering & Biotechnology News
notes: Excluded: robots.txt explicitly disallows anthropic-ai and Claude-Web.

name: STAT News (Pharma)
notes: Excluded: robots.txt explicitly disallows anthropic-ai and ClaudeBot. Also paywalled.

name: ConsultorSalud (Colombia)
notes: Colombian health-industry news portal; covers INVIMA approvals and LatAm pharma policy. No dedicated INVIMA feed was found, so this is the best available Colombia signal.

name: BioTuesdays (Canada)
notes: Canadian biotech/pharma trade press. Substitutes for Health Canada, whose actual news listing is not scrapable (see health-canada entry).

name: COFEPRIS (Mexico)
notes: No dedicated listing page was found (gob.mx/cofepris/articulos 404s), so this scrapes the homepage's press-release widget instead - only ~5 recent items per fetch. The secondary (non-featured) items show day+month but no year, so published_date is null for those; they still get a real date from fetched_at. Confirmed working from residential/dev networks, but returns a ~1.8KB HTTP 200 (vs the real ~880KB page) when fetched from Vercel's production deployment - almost certainly gob.mx treating the datacenter IP differently (bot-challenge/blank page). Left active since it's harmless to keep trying and may start working if that changes; last_error on the source will show '0 items matched' when it's happening.

name: DIGEMID (Peru)
notes: gob.pe's institutional page for DIGEMID 404s; digemid.minsa.gob.pe is DIGEMID's own WordPress site and has a working press-notes listing instead.

name: Pharmaceuticals and Medical Devices Agency (Japan)
notes: No working RSS feed found yet - needs a best-effort HTML fetch implementation before activating.

name: Health Canada
notes: Not scrapable: www.canada.ca's news pages render via client-side JS with an empty static HTML body, and the domain is fronted by bot management that stalls/blocks non-browser HTTP clients and hangs even a real headless browser. recalls-rappels.canada.ca is reachable but only covers recalls/safety alerts (the opposite signal from approvals). Using BioTuesdays as a substitute instead (see biotuesdays entry).

name: Therapeutic Goods Administration (Australia)
notes: Correction: tga.gov.au IS reachable (earlier 'unreachable' verdict was a curl-on-Windows/TLS artifact, not a real block - confirmed via Node fetch). Still needs a real htmlScrapeConfig (selectors not yet written) before activating.
